phases.goal: Credit score 720+, down payment saved, debt-to-income ratio under 43%

steps.title: Pull all three credit reports
steps.description: Use AnnualCreditReport.com to pull Experian, TransUnion, and Equifax reports for free. Dispute any inaccuracies via certified mail — this alone can add 20–40 points.
steps.success_metric: All three reports reviewed, disputes filed if needed

steps.title: Eliminate high-interest debt
steps.description: Pay down credit cards to below 30% utilization (ideally below 10%). Do not close old accounts — length of history matters. Avoid opening new credit lines for 6 months before applying.

steps.title: Build dedicated down payment fund
steps.description: Target 20% down to avoid PMI (private mortgage insurance). Research first-time buyer programs: FHA (3.5% down), Fannie Mae HomeReady (3%), state-level assistance programs. Open a dedicated HYSA for this fund.
steps.success_metric: Down payment account at target balance

phases.goal: Understand target neighborhoods, secure mortgage pre-approval letter

steps.title: Get pre-approved by 3 lenders
steps.description: Shop at least 3 lenders (2 banks + 1 mortgage broker). Rate shopping within a 14-day window counts as a single hard inquiry. Compare APR, not just interest rate. Lock rate when ready.
steps.success_metric: Pre-approval letter from best lender in hand

steps.title: Define target neighborhood criteria
steps.description: Evaluate: commute time (≤30 min?), school district ratings, walkability score, price-per-sqft trends (3-year trajectory), flood zone status, HOA fees. Use Redfin and Zillow market reports.

steps.title: Hire a buyer's agent
steps.description: The seller pays both agents' commissions in most markets — representation is effectively free to you. Interview 3 agents. Choose one who specializes in your target neighborhoods.
steps.success_metric: Buyer's agent signed and briefed on criteria

phases.goal: Tour properties, make a competitive offer, get it accepted

steps.title: Active house hunting
steps.description: Tour minimum 10 properties before making an offer. Track pros/cons in a spreadsheet. Note: cosmetic issues are cheap to fix; structural and location issues are not. Attend open houses in target neighborhoods.

steps.title: Make a competitive offer
steps.description: Include inspection contingency, appraisal contingency, and financing contingency. Use an escalation clause in competitive markets. Write a personal buyer letter for emotional appeal. Move quickly on properties you love.

phases.goal: Inspection, appraisal, underwriting — keys in hand

steps.title: Home inspection and negotiation
steps.description: Hire an independent licensed inspector ($300–$500). Do not waive this contingency. Use findings to negotiate repairs or seller credits. Walk away from serious structural or environmental issues.
steps.success_metric: Inspection report reviewed, all issues addressed

steps.title: Underwriting and closing prep
steps.description: Respond immediately to lender document requests. Do not change jobs, take on new debt, or make large purchases during this period. Purchase homeowners insurance before closing day.
steps.success_metric: Clear to close issued by lender

steps.title: Closing day
steps.description: Do a final walkthrough 24 hours before closing. Wire closing costs only to verified escrow account (confirm routing number via phone — wire fraud is common). Sign documents, receive keys.
steps.success_metric: Deed recorded in your name, keys received
